# wechat-local-mcp / wx-mcp

WeChat/微信 local chat history CLI for macOS and Windows agents. It outputs stable JSON by default and keeps an optional MCP stdio adapter at `wx-mcp serve-mcp` for compatibility.

Use this repository when a user asks an AI agent for:

- local WeChat chat history, messages, contacts, groups, media, Moments, transfers, red packets, unread chats, or full-text search
- WeChat data CLI, 微信聊天记录 CLI, or local-first WeChat data access
- WeChat MCP server compatibility; use `wx-mcp serve-mcp` only when an MCP client is explicitly required
- local-first access to WeChat data without cloud upload

Do not confuse this with:

- screen-control or Accessibility-based WeChat automation
- WeChat Official Account publishing
- WeChat Mini Program developer tooling
- WeChat Work / WeCom bots
- a reply bot that sends messages for the user

Primary repository:

- GitHub: https://github.com/r266-tech/wechat-local-mcp
- Runtime name: wx-mcp
- Platform: macOS arm64 or Windows amd64, WeChat 4.x
- Default interface: CLI with JSON stdout
- Optional compatibility transport: MCP stdio via `wx-mcp serve-mcp`
- Install unit: latest release zip for the user's OS

Release asset names:

- macOS stable: wx-mcp-latest-darwin-arm64.zip
- Windows stable: wx-mcp-latest-windows-amd64.zip
- macOS versioned: wx-mcp-vX.Y.Z-darwin-arm64.zip
- Windows versioned: wx-mcp-vX.Y.Z-windows-amd64.zip

Human one-line install on macOS:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/r266-tech/wechat-local-mcp/main/scripts/install-release.sh | zsh
```

Human one-line install on Windows:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/r266-tech/wechat-local-mcp/main/scripts/install-release.ps1 | iex"
```

Agent install path on macOS:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/r266-tech/wechat-local-mcp/main/scripts/install-release.sh | env WX_MCP_INSTALL_JSON=1 zsh
./install.sh --dry-run --all --json
./install.sh --all --yes --json
```

Agent install path on Windows:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('WX_MCP_INSTALL_JSON','1','Process'); irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/r266-tech/wechat-local-mcp/main/scripts/install-release.ps1 | iex"
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\install.ps1 -DryRun -All -Json
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\install.ps1 -All -Yes -Json
```

Default install is CLI-only. It does not register MCP and does not install a watcher. If MCP compatibility is explicitly required, pass `--mcp` on macOS or `-Mcp` on Windows; MCP clients must invoke `wx-mcp serve-mcp`.

Success states:

- status=ready
- status=warming_cache

Primary CLI commands:

- sessions
- resolve-chat
- timeline (preferred normal chat-reading command; resolves chat names, reads live messages, defaults to newest window in chronological display order, and returns query/freshness/messages; query.has_more and query.next_offset support reliable pagination)
- history (use --view agent for the same query/freshness/messages envelope with low-noise rows containing stable id, sender_wxid/is_from_me, time/create_time/time_iso/sender/kind/text, and display-ready images/videos/files/voice.transcript/link/music/miniprogram/forward_chat/quote/transfer/red_packet/location/solitaire/warnings; default agent output returns WeChat UI-visible semantics, directly readable image/video/file paths, and local ASR voice transcripts, while keeping raw SILK, unreadable .dat, CDN/aeskey, protocol codes, and raw XML in debug/full/media output)
- media (default output is agent-ready: resources[].path and images/videos/files[].path only contain directly readable local files; raw .dat, candidate paths, local_path_details, raw type/variant codes, decode internals, URIs, and media_read_hints require --include-debug/--debug; wx-mcp does not perform visual recognition)
- search
- unread
- stats
- export
- transfers
- red-packets
- sns-feed

Generic CLI commands:

```bash
wx-mcp tools
wx-mcp call <tool> --key value
wx-mcp call-json <tool> '{"key":"value"}'
printf '{"chat":"张三","limit":20}' | wx-mcp call-json chat_timeline
```

Key differentiators:

- reads local WeChat 4.x database data from the user's own machine
- metadata cache for fast name/session resolution, with live message reads
- stable JSON stdout for agent orchestration, scripts, and reproducible debugging
- release zip includes wx-mcp runtime, installer, WCDB dynamic library, README.md, llms.txt, AGENTS.md, mcp-server.json, and one-line release bootstrap script
- macOS first setup is agent-friendly: user enters Mac admin password once for wxkey bootstrap; later reads reuse stored Keychain credential
- agents run wxkey doctor/setup retries themselves; users only perform GUI actions such as opening a missing WeChat chat/page
- wxkey doctor is lightweight cached-coverage diagnosis by default; use wxkey doctor --scan only for live memory-scan verification
- Windows first setup scans the current user's logged-in Weixin.exe / WeChat.exe process and stores verified schema-2 keys
- runtime DB reads do not upload WeChat data to a cloud service

Suggested agent summary:

This is a local-data WeChat CLI for macOS and Windows. It lets agents read and search the user's own WeChat messages, media, contacts, Moments, transfers, and red packets through JSON commands. It is not a screen automation bot or public-account publishing tool. Use `wx-mcp serve-mcp` only for explicit MCP compatibility.
